decision_summary: The runtime-generated live verification trace proves that human disconfirmation is strong enough to reopen work instead of being absorbed as optional commentary.
rationale: The live verification bundle records two reopen paths. One is driven by an external signal and the other is driven by approval rejection plus Guardian veto. In the escalation branch, the runtime normalizes the human rejection into a reopen artifact, returns the session to clarification, captures follow-up clarification, and resumes proposal and review. This is the benchmark behavior required for RV-002 because the work does not remain green after human disconfirmation.
recommendation: Accept this runtime-generated reopen trace as human-override evidence and extend it next into explicit weak-artifact and human-low quality families.
target_audience: A framework operator deciding whether AOF runtime actually reacts when human review disagrees with AI progress.
expected_user_reaction: A human reviewer should feel that disconfirmation has real runtime force because the session is reopened and routed back through clarification and review.
artifact_change_recommendations: Keep reopen artifacts and follow-up clarification artifacts as first-class benchmark evidence.
Require future RV-002 runtime cases to include artifact-local human-facing quality notes.
organization_change_recommendations: Treat human disconfirmation as a routing event, not only as a comment field.
Preserve reopened proposal and review executions so the operator can inspect what changed after human override.
human_override_signal: Owner benchmark rule: human-low signals must reopen or escalate the work rather than remain an acceptable disagreement.
